MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Marjory Halligan, moving party/objector, appearing as a self-represented litigant.
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Ruling: Objector Marjory Halligan's Motion For Summary Judgment Of The Merits.

Matter called. The Court and Ms. Halligan confer regarding the issue of notice being provided to the other side of Ms. Halligan's intent to appear today and present argument. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court stated that it would proceed.

Ms. Halligan addressed a past petition for determination of trustor's intent. In addition, Ms. Halligan presented argument in support of the motion for summary judgment. The Court noted that the only matter before it today was the motion for summary judgment and at the conclusion of Ms. Halligan's argument adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

Objector Marjory Halligan's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED for the following reasons: (1) The Motion for Summary Judgment does not comply with the requirements of CCP 436; (2) there is no pleading on file to be summarily adjudicated; (3) Ms. Halligan has no standing under Probate Code 17200 to bring a motion concerning the internal affairs of this Trust; (4) this Court has previously ruled on the arguments raised in this pleading, and concluded that Ms. Halligan lacks standing in this matter and her claims are barred by the applicable statutes of limitations.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. Matter Not Reported.

Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer Of David Hillbrand, Executor Of The Will Of William James Shiloh Unruh, Decreased, To Petition For Order To Modify The Dispositive Provisions Of The Trust Filed By Marjory Halligan Ccp.430.10
Off Calendar Jan-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for David Hillbrand (respondent). Petitioner in Propria Persona Marjory Halligan.

Mr. Conrad argued that Marjory's petition which is her fourth is barred on 2 basis" 1. statute of limitations and 2. claim preclusions. Furthermore Mr. Conrad stated that there are no new facts, that Marjory claims the trustee did not give what should have been given to her, that Marjory is not a beneficiary and that Getty vs. Getty does not apply.

Marjory argued that Mr. Conrad does not provide any authority to modify a trust, that this is not a claim but a request and stated case background. The Court asked Marjory what is the applicable statue of limitations? Marjory argued that there is no statue of limitations. The Court cited 17200d(13) and 16460 and stated that in Getty vs. Getty there is a 3 year statue of limitations. Marjory replied that basically she ran out of time to modify. The Court stated that back in 2011 Judge Wiss sustained her petition without leave to amend, there is no statute of limitations that would apply to file this late, that the Court of Appeals ruled against her, that she was not ruled as a beneficiary and that she has no standings.

Regarding the vexatious litigant motion Mr. Conrad stated that access to the court is important but Marjory is filing endless litigation of the same claim. The Court stated that Marjory filed 2 petitions with an attorney and 2 on her own. Mr. Conrad argued that this same claim is draining the Court and estate resources. The Court stated that the motion for vexatious litigant is denied at this time but informs Marjory to take note that should she continue to file again the Court will consider deeming her a vexatious litigant.

The Court took the demurrer of David Hillbrand under submission. The Court denied the motion for order determining Majory Halligan to be a vexatious litigant (order to be prepared by the Court).

Judge John K. Stewart, Clerk: Nancy Wong, Bailiff: Deputy Malohney. Not Reported.

Demurrer Of David Hillbrand, Executor Of The Will Of William James Shiloh Unruh, Deceased, To Petition For Instructions By Marjory Halligan [Ccp 430.10]; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Sep-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Martin Kassman & Paul Melbostad for Marjory Halligan. Daniel Conrad for and with (moving party) David Hillbrand.

Mr. Conrad stated that the tentative ruling to sustain is correct, that they do not agree with allowing Ms. Halligan 30 days to amend, that the case is over, that details were provided in the points and authorities, that in 2012 there was a 3 day trial of the merits before Judge Wiss, that the statute of limitations was one of the key issues, that after trial the court ruled that the claim of the hand written document was time barred, that the trial took place 4.5 years after the death of Ms. Unruh, that Ms. Halligan had a long time to figure out the facts, that pages 8-14 of the statement of decision addressed the statute of limitations, that there was a full trial on the merits in which Ms. Halligan testified and called witnesses and her claim was found to be time barred. Mr. Conrad further stated that Ms. Halligan appealed the ruling and lost, that at least 1/2 of the trial was on the statute of limitations, that the cases Ms. Halligan cites do not apply, that 16460 is the correct statute in this case, that she has had a full chance in this court, a full hearing at the Court of Appeal and urges the court to reconsider changing its tentative ruling to without leave to amend. Mr. Kassman stated that if the demurrer is sustained there is no point in leave to amend, that the demurrer is not based on the statute of limitations, that Mr. Conrad cites no authority regarding the type of judgment, that if the basis is the statute of limitations then it does not have the res judicata effect, that provisions of the trust were litigated but not ruled on and that there was no judgment on the merits. Mr. Melbostad stated that they would like the case decided on the merits, that the issue is that the overriding purpose of probate it to carry out the wishes of the decedent, that the petitioner just asks the court if the hand written note of Ada is valid as an amendment to the trust, that they do not make allegations of breach of duty, that they do not mention Shiloh and that when the document was found it was given to Ms. Dolch. The Court asked if there would be a distinction if Judge Wiss ruled on the merits that it would be a carve out. Mr. Conrad stated that she did rule on the merits, that once the point was reached where the claim was denied the court did not need to get to the other merits and that the court found the claim not timely filed. The Court asked if there was a line of cases that suggest otherwise and if there is any trial case with a published opinion to such and that it will look at the issue more closely. The Court gave the parties until September 9, 2015 to submit additional authorities and the demurrer will be under submission as of that date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Court Repoter: Nancy E. Presant-McDonald, CSR 906, 800-288-3376 (Reported)
